origin.name: Romania
origin.key_document: Certificat de deces (death certificate from Stare Civila)
origin.source: FCDO Romania travel advice, updated 2025; British Embassy Bucharest guidance
origin.cremation_note: Cremation in Romania is available. Bringing ashes home to the UK is often simpler than full body repatriation. You will need the Certificat de deces, cremation certificate, and urn transport documentation.

destinations.name: United Kingdom
key_documents: Certificat de deces (death certificate from Stare Civila)
Certified English translation of death certificate
special_rules: Romania is an EU member and Hague Apostille Convention member, which simplifies document legalisation.
Death must be registered with the Stare Civila (Civil Status Office) within 3 days.
The Prosecutor's Office orders post-mortem for suspicious or violent deaths.
All Romanian-language documentation requires a certified English translation for UK acceptance.
destinations.dest_reception: The UK funeral director takes custody at the cargo terminal. All Romanian documentation must be in certified English translation. The coroner for the district is notified. Straightforward cases proceed directly to funeral arrangements.
destinations.dest_consular_note: FCDO 24-hour emergency line: +44 (0)20 7008 5000. The British Embassy in Bucharest can register the death and advise on local funeral directors. They cannot pay for or arrange repatriation.
destinations.seo_title: Romania to UK Repatriation: Guidance for Families
destinations.seo_description: Someone has died in Romania. Repatriation to the UK takes 1-2 weeks. Civil Status Office process, apostille, and documentation all handled. Contact us 24/7.
